content.full_text:
September 9, 1994

Anthony Cohen
Clement, Fitzpatrick & Kenworthy
P.O. Box 1494
Santa Rosa, CA  95402

Re: Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-94-274

Dear Mr. Cohen:

     This is in reply to your request for advice concerning
whether Nancy Gardner, a member of the board of directors of the
Westport County Water District of Mendocino County (the "District"), is permitted by the Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹
to participate in a particular decision before the board.

QUESTIONS

     1. If Ms. Gardner does not participate in a District
decision concerning her application for services from the
District, may she be counted toward the establishment of a quorum
so that the district can act on her application?

     2. If the answer to Question 1 is "no," what other avenues
are open to the District in order to act on Ms. Gardner's
application?

CONCLUSIONS

     1. Ms. Gardner may not be counted toward the establishment
of a quorum for the District to act on her application for
services.

     2. Under the facts presented, the Act permits no other
avenues for the District to act upon Ms. Gardner's application.
The District is legally permitted to act on the application
without Ms. Gardner's participation; the fact that one board
member may refuse to participate and thus eliminate the creation

___________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
Regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954. All references to regulations are to title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800  ●  P.O. Box 807  ●  Sacramento, CA  95804-0807  ●  (916) 322-5660

File No. A-94-274
Page 2


of a quorum is a political or legal issue outside the jurisdiction
of the Act.

FACTS

You are counsel for Nancy Gardner, a member of the board of
directors of the Westport County Water District of Mendocino
County.  By law, the district is required to have five board
members.

Currently, the board has only four members.  However, one of
these members has habitually missed board meetings and has
contributed on at least one occasion to the board's being unable
to conduct business due to a lack of a quorum.

Also, though Government Code Section 1780 provides several
alternative methods for filling board vacancies, the board has
been unable to elicit interest from anyone in filling the one
vacant board position.

A quorum of three members is necessary for the District to
conduct business.

Ms. Gardner owns real property within the District and has
submitted to the District an application for services.  She is
aware that, under the Act, she has a conflict of interest if she
participates in the decision concerning her application.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits a public official from making,
participating in making or using her official position to
influence a government decision in which she has a financial
interest.

Section 87103(b) states that an official has a financial
interest in a government decision when it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
effect on, among other things, real property in which the official
has an investment interest of $1,000 or more.

Regulation 18702.1(a)(3)(C) states that a government decision
has a "material" effect on a real property interest of an official
if the decision involves the imposition of a fee on the property.
Furthermore, Regulation 18702.1(a)(2)(B) finds a material effect
on real property when the decision involves the issuance or denial
of a license or permit concerning the use of real property.  Under
one or both of these provisions, it appears that the District's
decision on Ms. Gardner's application for services would have a
reasonably foreseeable material financial effect under the Act.

Despite the prohibition set forth in Section 87100, Section
87101 sets forth circumstances in which an official may

File No. A-94-274
Page 3


participate in a decision in which she has a financial interest.
Specifically, Section 87101 states:

     Section 87100 does not prevent any public official from
     making or participating in the making of a governmental
     decision to the extent his participation is legally
     required for the action or decision to be made. The
     fact that an official's vote is needed to break a tie
     does not make his participation legally required for
     purposes of this section.

     The Commission has adopted Regulation 18701 to interpret and
implement Section 87101. Subdivision (a) of Regulation 18701
states that Section 87101's exception does not apply where the
agency can legally make the decision by an alternative means.
Also, subdivision (c)(2) of the regulation states that the
exception does not apply "if a quorum can be convened of other
members of the agency who are not disqualified ... whether or not
such other members are actually present at the time of the
disqualification."

     Based on the facts presented, subdivision (c)(2) of
Regulation 18701 would apply, meaning that the legally required
participation exception of Section 87101 is not available to Ms.
Gardner. It appears that a quorum of the board can be achieved
without Ms. Gardner's participation if the member who is
habitually absent from meetings chooses to attend. This member's
failure to attend board meetings is not sufficient to create the
requisite legal necessity for Ms. Gardner's participation in the
decision despite having a conflict of interest. Resolution of the
problem created by the member's failure to attend meetings is a
political or legal issue which is beyond the jurisdiction of the
Act.2

     Should you have further questions, please contact me at
916/322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel

                                        By:_____________________
                                            Scott Maliibrin
                                            Assistant General Counsel

SGC:SH:ak

2  Since Section 87101's exception does not apply to Ms. Gardner
due to the availability of a third board member with whom a quorum
can be created, we do not reach the issue of whether the board's
inability to find a person interested in filling the vacant board
position triggers the exception.
